data_schema: id(int id, keep verbatim for submission); species(string target, 99 distinct leaf species — this is the label, present in train.csv only); margin1..margin64, shape1..shape64, texture1..texture64 (192 float features, pre-extracted image descriptors in [0,1]). test.csv has id + the 192 feature columns, no species.
extra_notes: Kaggle Leaf Classification. 99-class multiclass classification on 192 numeric features. Metric = multi-class log loss (LOWER is better); CV_SCORE = OOF/K-fold multiclass log loss. Only 990 train rows x 99 classes, so use StratifiedKFold and models that give calibrated probabilities (LogisticRegression, lightgbm multiclass, or a small MLP); watch for overfitting (regularize, keep it simple). submission.csv MUST have columns id + one probability column PER SPECIES named EXACTLY as in sample_submission.csv (id,Acer_Capillipes,...,Zelkova_Serrata), row order = test id order, each row's 99 probabilities sum to 1. metrics.json must contain {"cv_score": <logloss float>, "metric": "multiclass_log_loss"}.
